words.definition: to leave someone or something completely and not return
words.example: The captain refused to abandon ship until every passenger was safe.

words.definition: existing in large quantities; plentiful
words.example: Fresh fruit was abundant in the market during summer.

words.definition: familiar with something through frequent experience
words.example: She quickly became accustomed to her new school.

words.definition: enough for a particular purpose; satisfactory
words.example: We packed adequate supplies for the three-day hike.

words.definition: next to or adjoining something else
words.example: The library is adjacent to the school hall.

words.definition: able to move quickly and easily
words.example: The gymnast was remarkably agile on the balance beam.

words.definition: a person or group that helps and supports another
words.example: The two nations became allies during the war.

words.definition: more than enough; plentiful
words.example: There was ample time to finish the test.

words.definition: feeling worried or nervous
words.example: She felt anxious before her piano exam.

words.definition: clearly visible or understood; obvious
words.example: It was apparent that he had not studied for the test.

words.definition: anxious about something that might happen
words.example: He was apprehensive about starting his new school.

words.definition: having very little rainfall; extremely dry
words.example: Few plants can survive in the arid desert.

words.definition: to climb or move upwards
words.example: The hikers began to ascend the steep mountain path.

words.definition: genuine and real, not a copy
words.example: The museum displayed an authentic Roman coin.

words.definition: to send someone away as a punishment
words.example: The king ordered the traitor to be banished from the land.

words.definition: kind and generous
words.example: The benevolent old man donated his fortune to the orphanage.

words.definition: confused; puzzled
words.example: The tourists looked bewildered by the confusing map.

words.definition: noisy, energetic, and cheerful
words.example: The boisterous crowd cheered loudly at the match.

words.definition: hard but easily broken
words.example: The old, brittle pages of the book crumbled at the edges.

words.definition: honest and straightforward
words.example: She gave a candid answer about her mistake.

words.definition: having the ability to do something well
words.example: He is a capable leader of the team.

words.definition: careful to avoid danger or mistakes
words.example: Be cautious when crossing the busy road.

words.definition: to stop or bring to an end
words.example: The noise finally ceased after midnight.

words.definition: a remarkable occurrence of events by chance
words.example: Meeting my cousin abroad was a complete coincidence.

words.definition: extremely large
words.example: The colossal statue towered over the city square.

words.definition: sympathy and concern for the suffering of others
words.example: She treated every patient with great compassion.

words.definition: to understand fully
words.example: It took a while to comprehend the difficult instructions.

words.definition: to hide something from view
words.example: He tried to conceal his disappointment with a smile.

words.definition: giving information clearly in few words
words.example: Her concise report covered all the key points.

words.definition: to express strong disapproval of something
words.example: The teacher condemned the pupils' rude behaviour.

words.definition: to tell someone a secret, trusting them
words.example: She confided her worries to her best friend.

words.definition: overcrowding that causes a blockage
words.example: Traffic congestion delayed the school bus by an hour.

words.definition: careful to do things correctly and thoroughly
words.example: He is a conscientious student who checks his work twice.

words.definition: notably large in size or amount
words.example: The renovation cost a considerable sum of money.

words.definition: clearly visible; attracting attention
words.example: Her bright red coat made her conspicuous in the crowd.

words.definition: to say the opposite of a statement
words.example: The witness's account seemed to contradict the evidence.

words.definition: a disagreement causing strong public argument
words.example: The new law caused great controversy among voters.

words.definition: fitting in well with a person's needs
words.example: The new bus stop is very convenient for school.

words.definition: warm and friendly
words.example: They exchanged cordial greetings at the meeting.

words.definition: polite and considerate
words.example: The courteous waiter helped the elderly couple to their seats.

words.definition: clever at deceiving people
words.example: The cunning fox tricked the crow into dropping its cheese.

words.definition: to make someone believe something untrue
words.example: The advert was designed to deceive customers.

words.definition: to politely refuse; or to gradually decrease
words.example: She had to decline the invitation due to illness.

words.definition: openly resisting authority
words.example: The defiant toddler refused to put on his shoes.

words.definition: lacking in some necessary quality
words.example: The soil was deficient in essential nutrients.

words.definition: done on purpose; intentional
words.example: It was a deliberate act of kindness.

words.definition: to destroy or tear down completely
words.example: The old factory was demolished to make way for flats.

words.definition: to state that something is not true
words.example: He continued to deny any involvement in the prank.

words.definition: to move downward
words.example: The plane began to descend towards the runway.

words.definition: the complete loss of hope
words.example: She felt a wave of despair when she lost her ticket.

words.definition: to feel intense dislike for
words.example: He despised the way the bully treated younger children.

words.definition: to become progressively worse
words.example: The weather began to deteriorate rapidly during the climb.

words.definition: to cause severe damage or great distress
words.example: The storm devastated the coastal village.

words.definition: showing a skill for achieving goals in an underhand way
words.example: He used a devious trick to win the game unfairly.

words.definition: showing calm self-control and seriousness
words.example: She gave a dignified speech despite her disappointment.

words.definition: showing careful and persistent effort
words.example: The diligent student revised for weeks before the exam.

words.definition: to become or make smaller
words.example: Her enthusiasm did not diminish despite the setback.

words.definition: to get rid of something no longer wanted
words.example: He discarded the broken toy in the bin.

words.definition: to make secret information known
words.example: The company had to disclose its financial troubles.

words.definition: careful not to attract attention; tactful
words.example: She was discreet about her friend's personal problem.

words.definition: depressing; lacking hope or cheer
words.example: The weather was dismal for the entire holiday.

words.definition: to send off promptly for a purpose
words.example: The general dispatched troops to defend the border.

words.definition: a disagreement or argument
words.example: The neighbours had a dispute over the garden fence.

words.definition: clearly different or noticeable
words.example: The two twins had very distinct personalities.

words.definition: showing a great deal of variety
words.example: The city has a wonderfully diverse population.

words.definition: most important, powerful, or influential
words.example: The lion is the dominant animal in the pride.

words.definition: hesitating or doubting; not to be trusted
words.example: I was dubious about the stranger's offer.

words.definition: unconventional and slightly strange
words.example: Our eccentric neighbour collects vintage teapots.

words.definition: strange and frightening
words.example: An eerie silence filled the abandoned house.

words.definition: involving many carefully arranged parts or details
words.example: The wedding had an elaborate decoration scheme.

words.definition: having the right to do or receive something
words.example: Only students over eleven are eligible for the trip.

words.definition: fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing
words.example: The eloquent speaker moved the whole audience.

words.definition: to begin a journey or new venture
words.example: The explorers embarked on their voyage at dawn.

words.definition: famous and respected within a particular field
words.example: The eminent scientist won a Nobel Prize.

words.definition: to try hard to achieve something
words.example: She endeavoured to finish the marathon despite the pain.

words.definition: to suffer something difficult patiently; to last
words.example: The old bridge has endured a hundred years of storms.

words.definition: very large in size or amount
words.example: An enormous whale swam beside the boat.

words.definition: intense and eager enjoyment or interest
words.example: The team greeted the news with great enthusiasm.

words.definition: feeling discontented because of another's advantages
words.example: She felt envious of her sister's new bicycle.

words.definition: equal in value, amount, or meaning
words.example: One kilometre is roughly equivalent to 0.6 miles.

words.definition: not consistent or predictable
words.example: The car's erratic driving alarmed other motorists.

words.definition: absolutely necessary
words.example: Water is essential for every living creature.

words.definition: to escape or avoid, especially cleverly
words.example: The thief managed to evade the police for weeks.

words.definition: clearly seen or understood; obvious
words.example: It was evident that she had practised hard.

words.definition: to represent something as bigger than it really is
words.example: He tends to exaggerate how far he ran.

words.definition: to be exceptionally good at something
words.example: She excels in both mathematics and music.

words.definition: to drain someone's energy completely
words.example: The long hike exhausted the whole group.

words.definition: spending or costing much more than necessary
words.example: The extravagant party cost a small fortune.

words.definition: possible to do easily or conveniently
words.example: Is it feasible to finish the project by Friday?

words.definition: savagely fierce or violent
words.example: The ferocious storm tore roofs off houses.

words.definition: to grow or develop successfully
words.example: The garden flourished after the spring rain.

words.definition: easily broken or damaged
words.example: Handle the fragile vase with great care.

words.definition: open and honest in expression
words.example: She was frank about her reasons for leaving.

words.definition: careful with money; not wasteful
words.example: His frugal habits helped him save for university.

words.definition: extremely angry
words.example: Dad was furious when he saw the broken window.

words.definition: pointless; producing no useful result
words.example: It was futile to argue with the stubborn judge.

words.definition: willing to give more than is necessary
words.example: The generous donor gave money to every local school.

words.definition: truly what it is said to be; sincere
words.example: Her genuine smile put everyone at ease.

words.definition: courteous, kind, and pleasant
words.example: The gracious host welcomed every guest warmly.

words.definition: the quality of being thankful
words.example: She wrote a letter to express her gratitude.

words.definition: causing horror or disgust
words.example: The film contained several gruesome scenes.

words.definition: risky; dangerous
words.example: Climbing the cliff without ropes was hazardous.

words.definition: slow to act because of uncertainty
words.example: He was hesitant to raise his hand in class.

words.definition: unfriendly and aggressive
words.example: The dog gave a hostile growl at the stranger.

words.definition: having a modest view of one's own importance
words.example: Despite winning, she remained humble about her success.
